clinical_coherence.notes: 62M with DM2, chronic sinusitis, and baseline right hearing loss presenting with classic meningitis triad. Recent sinusitis exacerbation treated with amoxicillin-clavulanate provides a clear source. The age-appropriate comorbidities and realistic medication list (metformin, glipizide, lisinopril, fluticasone) are clinically plausible.

tool_output_consistency.notes: CSF WBC 3069 with 94% neutrophils, protein 363.4, glucose 15.3, positive Gram stain for Gram-positive diplococci. MRI with leptomeningeal enhancement, sulcal DWI restriction, and sinus disease. All outputs perfectly concordant with acute pneumococcal meningitis.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G00.1 correct. Critical actions and contraindicated actions are clinically appropriate. Key reasoning points correctly address the sinusitis-meningitis connection and the importance of adding age-appropriate Listeria coverage (ampicillin) for patient >50.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Textbook meningitis triad, positive Gram stain, extreme CSF findings. Appropriately labeled straightforward.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 62-year-old Hispanic male with DM2 and chronic sinusitis. Age >50 adds the teaching point of Listeria coverage. Highly realistic demographic.

educational_value.notes: Excellent teaching case: age >50 mandates empiric Listeria coverage (ampicillin), recent antibiotic use for sinusitis is the infection source, and the hearing loss provides clinical context. The age-based antibiotic modification is a key learning point.

strengths: Age >50 introduces Listeria coverage teaching point
Clear pathophysiologic connection between sinusitis and meningitis
Detailed, realistic medication and PMH profiles
Outstanding educational value for empiric antibiotic selection
